2606.25389.title: Offline Multi-agent Continual Cooperation via Skill Partition and Reuse
2606.25389.paper_summary: Extracting skills from multi-agent offline dataset improves learning efficiency via sharing task-invariant coordination skills among tasks. In settings where tasks occur sequentially and the space of skills grows exponentially, existing approaches that rely on heuristically designed and fixed-sized skill libraries struggle to resolve the problem of distributional shift and interference, facing catastrophic forgetting and plasticity loss. To address this problem and endow agents with the ability to continually discover and reuse coordination skills in open-environment, we propose COMAD, a principled framework for Continual Offline Multi-agent Skill Discovery via Skill Partition and Reuse. We first discover skills from mixed multi-agent behavior data with an auto-encoder to transform coordination knowledge into reusable coordination skills. Then we construct a skill-augmented policy learning objective with multi-head architectures, explicitly guiding the advantage function with reusable skills identified via a density-based reusability estimator. Theoretical analysis shows our method approximates the optimum of a continual skill discovery problem. Empirical results across diverse MARL benchmarks show that COMAD continually expands its skill library to mitigate interference, achieving superior forward and backward transfer for task streams compared to multiple baselines.
2606.25389.paper_summary_zh: 提取多智能體離線數據集中的技能透過在任務之間共享任務不變的協調技能來提高學習效率。 在任務按順序發生且技能空間指數增長的情況下，依賴於啟發式設計和固定大小技能庫的現有方法難以解決分佈轉移和干擾問題，面臨災難性遺忘和可塑性損失。 為了解決這個問題並賦予智能體在開放環境中持續發現和重用協調技能的能力，我們提出了COMAD，一個基於原則的持續離線多智能體技能發現框架，通過技能劃分和重用。 我們首先使用自編碼器從混合多智能體行為數據中發現技能，將協調知識轉化為可重用的協調技能。 然後，我們構建了一個技能增強的策略學習目標，使用多頭架構，明確指導優勢函數，並利用基於密度的可重用性估計器識別的可重用技能。 理論分析顯示我們的方法近似於持續技能發現問題的最優解。 在多樣的MARL基準測試中的實證結果顯示，COMAD持續擴展其技能庫以減輕干擾，實現了相較於多個基準的任務流的優越前向和後向轉移。
2606.25389.author: Yuchen Xiao et.al.
2606.25389.authors: Yuchen Xiao, Lei Yuan, Ruiqi Xue, Tieyue Yin, Yang Yu
